content.text:
<user_query>
cancel this rebase, checkout the branch again pls
</user_query>

Checking git state to cancel the rebase and restore the branch.

[REDACTED]

input.command: cd /workspaces/agno && git status && git branch -v && git rebase --show-current-patch 2>/dev/null | head -5 || true
input.description: Check git status and rebase state

input.command: cd /workspaces/agno && ls -la .git/rebase-* 2>/dev/null; git reflog -15; echo "---"; git log --oneline -5 HEAD; echo "---"; git log --oneline -5 origin/coder/issue-131
input.description: Check rebase dirs, reflog, and branch tips

input.command: cd /workspaces/agno && git reset --hard origin/coder/issue-131 && git status && git log --oneline -5
input.description: Reset branch to pre-rebase remote state

content.text:
Rebase is undone. The rebase had already finished, so I reset `coder/issue-131` to match `origin/coder/issue-131` at `2b5dc6a` — the state before the rebase onto `main`.

You’re on `coder/issue-131`, in sync with the remote, with a clean working tree. The 9 rebased commits that had been on top of `main` are gone from this branch (still in reflog if you need them).

[REDACTED]
